svg.text: render_size: 640×360 · 3 composited planes · transparent HUD overlay on top · z: stars=0 planets=1 game=2 hud=10
Stars — static dots + slow drift. Pure decoration, never collide.
Planets — large vector shapes, parallax scroll, no gameplay role.
anchor: tl · pad: 8px · no bg_colour · no border · no shadow
label: Orbitron:ascii hud_label · value: generic:2 hud_value
anchor: tr · pad: 8px · no bg_colour · right-aligned text
label: Orbitron:ascii hud_label · value: generic:2 hud_value
Panel widget fills with Color::DarkGrey when bg_colour is omitted.
The 4 HUD corner panels render opaque boxes over stars + planets + game.
Change fallback in sprite_renderer.rs: Color::DarkGrey → Color::Reset.
Skip writing interior cells when panel_bg == Color::Reset (preserve layers below).
game-over overlay uses explicit bg_colour — unaffected.
Each plane is a separate named layer in scene.yml (ref: game/layers/*.yml).
Planets layer: large vector circles + optional parallax offset via Rhai each frame.
Stars layer: small vector points or pre-placed text sprites, very slow drift.
hud_value (#f6ae2d) high-contrast against all 3 dark plane tones.
Planets are dim (#0a1230 ~4% brightness) — HUD text stays readable above them.
⚠ If planets get bright rings, add per-glyph shadow/outline to HUD labels.
shell-engine · asteroids mod · HUD design v2 · docs/layout/hud-design.svg
640×360 · 3-plane composite · halfblock terminal renderer · taffy grid
